name: Acorn Squash Soup
recipeIngredient: 4 1/2 lb acorn squash, halved (5 tbsp butter, unsalted 4 large leeks, chopped (white and tender green) 7 thyme, fresh or 1 tsp dried thyme 5 cups chicken broth 1 1/4 tsp salt 1/2 tsp pepper, freshly ground 1/2 cup sour cream 2 tbsp chives, chopped (3 tbsp total) 8 bacon slices, fried crisp and crumbled
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Place squash cut side down on a baking sheet and bake until tender, about 40 minutes. Let cool slightly. Using a spoon, scoop out the flesh.
2. In a large heavy saucepan, melt the butter over low heat. Add the chopped leeks and thyme. Cook, stirring constantly, until soft and lightly browned. Remove thyme sprigs (if using fresh).
3. Stir in the stock and the squash. Simmer for around 20 minutes. In small batches, place in blender or food processor. Puree until smooth. Pour back into the pan and season with salt and pepper.
4. Serve with a dollop of sour cream and crumbled bacon.
